full_response:
Assumptions and notation  
‣  Arthur’s inertial frame  S=(t ,x) :  
 Knight #1 starts at x=0 and rides in the +x–direction with speed v.  
 Knight #2 starts at x=L and rides in the −x–direction with the same speed v.  
 At t=0 the two knights leave their respective starting lines simultaneously in S.  

‣  Knight #2’s rest frame S₂=(t₂ ,x₂) is obtained from S by  
 (i) a translation that puts the common origin of the two frames at the event  
  O : (t=0 , x=L)  (the instant Knight #2 leaves his own starting line), and  
 (ii) a Lorentz boost with speed −v (so that Knight #2 is at rest in S₂).  

 β ≡ v/c (β>0) , γ ≡ 1/√(1−β²)  

The event we are interested in  
E : “Knight #1 crosses his own starting line.”  
In Arthur’s frame this occurs at  
 (t_E , x_E) = (0 , 0).

Step 1 – subtract the translational offset  
Relative to the origin O (t=0 , x=L) the event E has  
 Δt = t_E − 0 = 0,  Δx = x_E − L = −L.

Step 2 – Lorentz-transform the interval (Δt , Δx)  
Because S₂ moves with velocity −v with respect to S, the boost parameter is β’ = −β.  
Using the standard Lorentz transformation

 t₂ = γ ( Δt − β’ Δx /c ) ,  x₂ = γ ( Δx − β’ cΔt )

and inserting Δt = 0 , Δx = −L :

 t₂ = γ [ 0 − (−β)(−L) /c ] = γ ( −βL /c ) = −γ β L /c ,  
 x₂ = γ ( −L − 0 ) = −γ L.

Result  
In Knight #2’s frame the crossing of Knight #1 with his own starting line occurs

 (t₂ , x₂) = ( −γ β L /c ,  −γ L ).

Thus Knight #1 crosses his start line a time

 |t₂| = γ β L /c

earlier than Knight #2’s “start-time” (t₂=0), and at a position γL behind Knight #2.

\boxed{(t_{2},\,x_{2})=\left(-\gamma\,\beta\,\dfrac{L}{c},\;-\gamma L\right)}, with β=\dfrac{v}{c},\;\gamma=\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{1-\beta^{2}}}. 

Confidence: 69%
